rationale: The cited paper is referenced in grouped citations to support a general background claim that utilitarian products are practical and have more easily justified worth. In the citing paper, this serves as conceptual context for selecting utilitarian items in a follow-up study, but the authors do not engage the cited paper's specific acquisition-versus-forfeiture mechanism, findings, or methods. The core theoretical and empirical contribution of the citing paper (price primacy and neural valuation dynamics) does not depend directly on this specific source.
